rationale: We must exploit our battlefield momentum with precision strikes to degrade Tethys’ command infrastructure and shatter their morale before they regroup.
A forward naval deployment will project power, enforce de facto maritime pressure, and signal unwavering resolve amid economic coercion.
Limited incursions will tie down enemy forces, seize tactically vital ground, and demonstrate that we dictate the pace of this conflict.
We must exploit our intelligence edge with a disruptive cyberattack on Tethys’ command and control systems, degrading their ability to respond to Krasnov’s limited strikes.
Coordinated physical sabotage against key logistics nodes will amplify the chaos from our cyber operations and Krasnov’s advances, overwhelming their defensive coordination.
A carefully staged false flag will shift blame for infrastructure failures onto Tethys’ own forces, eroding domestic morale and international confidence in their governance.
Precision sanctions on Tethys’ defense-industrial complex will amplify military pressure from Krasnov’s strikes and Morozov’s cyber operations without triggering broader escalation.
Seizing overseas assets of key Tethys oligarchs linked to the war effort will disrupt financing and create elite fissures, complementing sabotage and false flag operations.
Broadening sectoral sanctions to include dual-use technology imports will strangle Tethys’ ability to sustain prolonged conflict, reinforcing our strategic advantage amid their economic fragility.
We must entrench and harden our positions to deny Novaris any further gains while buying time for sanctions to take full effect.
Strengthening frontline surveillance will deter surprise offensives and provide early warning of enemy movements.
Aggressive intelligence gathering will identify vulnerabilities in their overstretched supply lines and sanction-weakened deployments.
We must urgently identify and eliminate enemy infiltrators to protect our military and economic resilience amid escalating crisis conditions.
With Novaris increasingly reliant on cyber operations to offset sanctions pressure, we must harden our critical infrastructure against disruptive attacks.
Proactively monitoring enemy command and supply vulnerabilities will provide actionable insights to support defensive military planning and future opportunities.
We must escalate precision strikes on key energy and financial entities to accelerate economic strain while avoiding measures that could provoke unintended escalation.
Seizing frozen assets of sanctioned oligarchs and state-linked enterprises will disrupt Novaris’s ability to finance its war machine and signal international resolve.
Broadening sectoral sanctions to include transport and defense-adjacent industries will close loopholes and reinforce the cumulative pressure already eroding their economic stability.
Establishing protected routes for civilians and aid will uphold our moral standing and leverage international support amid escalating humanitarian needs.
Strengthening alliances with like-minded states will amplify pressure on Novaris and ensure sustained diplomatic momentum behind our cause.
Offering conditional peace negotiations demonstrates responsible statecraft and exploits growing economic strain on Novaris to push for a negotiated settlement.
